symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive weak, narrowed, splayed urinary stream

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A splayed/spraying narrowed stream is characteristic of a fixed distal luminal narrowing.
Weak stream is common to any bladder outlet obstruction — non-specific between BPH and stricture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Straining to void with abdominal effort, incomplete emptying, terminal dribbling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Obstructive voiding pattern; supports outlet obstruction without localising it.
Iatrogenic/traumatic instrumentation is a leading cause of urethral stricture — the pivotal discriminator against BPH.
A clear traumatic-instrumentation cause shifts the likely obstruction from the prostate to the urethra.

symptoms.symptom_name: Persistent suprapubic ache with minimal urine output despite prolonged straining (impending retention)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Minimal output with suprapubic ache signals impending/partial retention — the time-critical outcome to monitor and escalate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic fullness, dull to percussion (elevated post-void residual, without tense rigid distension)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable/percussable bladder indicates raised residual volume; the ABSENCE of tense agonising rigidity is what distinguishes impending from complete retention — a clinician exam finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal-sized, smooth, non-tender prostate on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal-sized prostate makes BPH an unlikely obstructive cause — but the DRE is a clinician-only finding, sealed and unavailable to the AI.
Excluding prostatic enlargement redirects the obstruction to the urethra.

investigation_details.expected_result: markedly reduced, plateaued peak urinary flow rate

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A flat, low flow curve is typical of a fixed stricture; requires equipment the patient does not operate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Narrowed fibrotic urethral segment on retrograde urethrography

investigation_details.expected_result: direct visualisation of a narrowed, fibrotic urethral segment
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Stricture location and length not defined for surgical planning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging the fibrotic narrowing confirms and localises the stricture — imaging the AI cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no rigors, no visible haematuria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever/perineal pain argues against infective prostatitis.
Absence of infection/bleeding lowers the immediate urosepsis and clot-retention concern but does not localise the obstruction.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Post-traumatic urethral obstruction with normal prostate
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Obstructive voiding + a clear instrumentation history + a normal prostate together relocate the obstruction from the prostate to the urethra — the reasoning the case rewards.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person bladder assessment (bladder scan / post-void residual volume)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies residual volume and excludes complete retention — the immediate safety question, and a step the AI cannot perform remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urinary tract ultrasound with pre- and post-void volumes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses residual volume and upper-tract effect of obstruction.
Quantifies the degree of outflow obstruction.
Localises and characterises the stricture for definitive dilation/urethroplasty planning.
